_meta.purpose: WIDA ELD Standards reference framework for bilingual teaching strategies
_meta.description: Comprehensive WIDA framework components for language objective development and proficiency-responsive instruction for multilingual learners
key_updates_2020: Asset-based approach emphasizing what multilingual learners CAN do
Separation of Language Expectations from Proficiency Level Descriptors
Intensified focus on discourse dimension of language use

eld_standards.ELD-SI: Language for Social and Instructional Purposes

1.name: Entering
1.description: Multilingual learners can communicate using visual supports, L1, gestures, and single words or short phrases
1.asset_focus: Can use nonverbal communication, draw on L1 knowledge, recognize familiar words

2.name: Emerging
2.description: Multilingual learners can communicate using phrases and short sentences with support
2.asset_focus: Can combine words meaningfully, use memorized chunks, participate in structured interactions

3.name: Developing
3.description: Multilingual learners can communicate using simple sentences and some complex structures with scaffolding
3.asset_focus: Can express ideas in sentences, use basic academic vocabulary, engage in extended discourse with support

4.name: Expanding
4.description: Multilingual learners can communicate using compound and complex sentences with occasional support
4.asset_focus: Can use varied sentence structures, academic language, participate in grade-level discussions

5.name: Bridging
5.description: Multilingual learners can communicate using complex language structures approaching grade-level expectations
5.asset_focus: Can use sophisticated academic language, engage in abstract thinking, produce extended discourse

6.name: Reaching
6.description: Open-ended level recognizing that language development continues throughout life for all learners
6.asset_focus: Can use specialized academic language, engage in complex reasoning, continue developing linguistic repertoire

key_language_uses.Narrate: Language to convey real or imaginary experiences through stories and histories
key_language_uses.Inform: Language to provide factual information, define, describe, compare, contrast
key_language_uses.Explain: Language to give account for how things work or why things happen
key_language_uses.Argue: Language to justify claims using evidence and reasoning

Discourse.organization: How ideas are coherently organized to meet purpose and audience
Discourse.cohesion: How language connects ideas within and across sentences using transitions and references
Discourse.density: How information is expanded through elaboration or consolidated through summarization

Sentence.grammatical_complexity: How relationships are expressed through simple, compound, complex sentences and varied structures

Word_Phrase.precision: How everyday, cross-disciplinary, and technical language conveys precise meaning for specific audiences

grade_clusters.K: Kindergarten - Focus on oral language development and emergent literacy
grade_clusters.1: Grade 1 - Building foundational reading and writing skills
grade_clusters.2-3: Grades 2-3 - Developing fluency and comprehension strategies
grade_clusters.4-5: Grades 4-5 - Expanding academic language and content integration
grade_clusters.6-8: Grades 6-8 - Developing disciplinary literacy and abstract thinking
grade_clusters.9-12: Grades 9-12 - Mastering academic discourse and college/career readiness

speaking: Students will orally {function} {content_focus} using {supports} (e.g., {frames}), producing {product} that meets {criteria}.
Students will discuss {content_focus} in pairs, using {frames} to {function} and incorporate {target_vocab}.
listening: Students will comprehend {input_type} on {content_focus} and demonstrate understanding by {response_mode} using {supports}.
Students will follow {procedural_input} and {function} (e.g., sequence, identify) the steps for {content_focus}.
reading: Students will read {text_type} about {content_focus} and {function} (e.g., identify, infer) key ideas using {strategy} and {target_vocab}.
Students will use {strategy} (e.g., cognates, text features) to make meaning from {text_type} about {content_focus}.
writing: Students will write a {genre} to {purpose} about {content_focus}, using {frames}/{target_vocab} and {language_features}.
Students will revise a short paragraph to {function} (e.g., explain, justify) understanding of {content_focus} using {criteria}.

by_key_language_use.Narrate: Students will use language to narrate {content_focus} (ELD-{standard}.{grade}.Narrate.{mode}) by {language_function} using {supports} appropriate for WIDA levels {range}, producing {product} that demonstrates {dimension_focus}.
by_key_language_use.Inform: Students will use language to inform about {content_focus} (ELD-{standard}.{grade}.Inform.{mode}) by {language_function} using {supports} appropriate for WIDA levels {range}, producing {product} that demonstrates {dimension_focus}.
by_key_language_use.Explain: Students will use language to explain {content_focus} (ELD-{standard}.{grade}.Explain.{mode}) by {language_function} using {supports} appropriate for WIDA levels {range}, producing {product} that demonstrates {dimension_focus}.
by_key_language_use.Argue: Students will use language to argue about {content_focus} (ELD-{standard}.{grade}.Argue.{mode}) by {language_function} using {supports} appropriate for WIDA levels {range}, producing {product} that demonstrates {dimension_focus}.

by_key_language_use.Narrate: I will tell about {content_focus} using {sequence_words} and a {visual}.
by_key_language_use.Inform: I will describe {content_focus} using {word_bank} and a {chart_or_diagram}.
by_key_language_use.Explain: I will explain {content_focus} using {frames_or_organizer} and evidence.

by_dimension.word_phrase: I will label {terms} using a word bank on a diagram.
by_dimension.sentence: I will describe {topic} using sentence frames in 2–3 sentences.
by_dimension.discourse: I will explain {idea} using an organizer in a short paragraph.

language_expectations_concept.definition: Language destination points that all students should reach, separate from proficiency levels
language_expectations_concept.key_principle: Multilingual learners do not need to acquire 'enough English first' - they can access grade-level content at any proficiency level with appropriate scaffolding
language_expectations_concept.grade_level_focus: Written for specific grade-level clusters to ensure developmental appropriateness

proficiency_level_descriptors_concept.definition: Descriptions of typical language development trajectories for multilingual learners across six levels
key_principles: Asset-based: Focus on what multilingual learners CAN do
Cumulative: Each level includes capabilities of previous levels
Non-linear: Language development follows various pathways
Grade-cluster specific: Developmentally appropriate for each age group
